publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Decomposition; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Decomposition: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Decomposition means writing many tiny functions even when their responsibilities and interfaces are unclear.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. A good decomposition names responsibilities and data flow so each part can be understood and tested while the parts still cover the complete requirement.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. A good decomposition names responsibilities and data flow so each part can be understood and tested while the parts still cover the complete requirement.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Decomposition instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: A good decomposition names responsibilities and data flow so each part can be understood and tested while the parts still cover the complete requirement.
Decomposition means writing many tiny functions even when their responsibilities and interfaces are unclear.

answer.explanation: A good decomposition names responsibilities and data flow so each part can be understood and tested while the parts still cover the complete requirement.
